title: Kissimmee River Wild and Scenic River Act
short_title: Kissimmee River Wild and Scenic River Act
plain_summary: This bill protects a 31-mile section of the Kissimmee River in Florida by adding it to a special group of rivers called the Wild and Scenic Rivers System. The protected part of the river runs between Lake Kissimmee and Lake Okeechobee. The government will take care of this river area and make sure no one can mine or build on the federal land around it. This helps keep the river clean and natural for people to enjoy for recreation.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Kissimmee River Wild and Scenic River Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Designation of Wild and Scenic River Segment, Kissimmee River, Florida
sections.text: This section changes the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act by adding the Kissimmee River in Florida to the list of protected rivers. The protected part of the river starts about sixteen miles downstream from Lake Kissimmee and ends about fifteen miles upstream from Lake Okeechobee. This is the section of the river that has been restored to its natural condition. The Secretary of the Interior will manage this river section as a recreational river, which means people can use it for activities like boating and fishing while it stays protected. Any federal land within the boundaries of this protected river section is withdrawn from certain uses. This means that as long as people do not already have legal rights to use the land, no one can claim ownership of the land under public land laws, no one can mine there under mining laws, and no one can lease the land for minerals or geothermal energy under mineral leasing laws.
